skill.name: vue-best-practices
skill.description: Vue 3 best-practice guidance for TypeScript, vue-tsc, Volar, component typing, strict templates, router params, Pinia tests, and SSR HMR.
skill.summary: Apply focused Vue 3 TypeScript, Volar, and vue-tsc best practices.
skill.author: hyf0

security_audit.summary: AI review found a documentation-only Vue skill with no executable files, no credential access, no data exfiltration, and no prompt injection attempts. Most static findings are false positives from markdown code fences, ordinary documentation links, and Vue or TypeScript prose; a few shell examples should be treated as user-reviewed developer commands.

low_findings.title: Developer Shell Commands Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Status: NEEDS_REVIEW. The skill includes shell examples such as npm installs and a Nuxt cache cleanup command. They are inside markdown guidance, not executable skill code, but users should review destructive cleanup commands before running them. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: The commands appear in Vue troubleshooting documentation with scoped project paths and no network exfiltration, but rm -rf remains operational if copied into a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are markdown examples for Vue development workflows, not code executed by the skill. The scoped rm -rf cleanup command still warrants user review before execution.

low_findings.title: Static Execution Findings Are Markdown Fence False Positives
low_findings.description: Status: FALSE_POSITIVE. The analyzer reported many Ruby backtick execution hits, but the reviewed locations are markdown code fences containing Vue, TypeScript, JSON, bash, and configuration examples. No executable Ruby or script file is present. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The flagged files are all .md rule documents and the backticks delimit examples rather than runtime code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static pattern matches markdown code fences across rule files. There is no evidence of Ruby backtick execution or an executable loader in the skill.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Are Not Runtime Network Activity
low_findings.description: Status: FALSE_POSITIVE. Hardcoded URL findings are reference links to Vue, Vite, Pinia, TypeScript, and related project documentation. The skill has no runtime networking code. Confidence: 0.98. Confidence reasoning: The URLs are markdown references in SKILL.md and rule documents, with no fetch, curl exfiltration flow, webhook, or credential transfer.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every reviewed URL is a documentation reference. No code path makes network requests or sends local data to those URLs.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Reconnaissance Hits Are Semantic False Positives
low_findings.description: Status: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static high-risk blocker hits point to markdown front matter, Vue option names, prose, or file extensions rather than cryptographic algorithms or system reconnaissance. No hashing, credential discovery, host enumeration, or suspicious environment inspection was found. Confidence: 0.93. Confidence reasoning: Reviewed locations are documentation text and examples for Vue configuration, not security-sensitive code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scanner appears to match substrings such as md, node, or system in markdown content. No evidence of weak crypto use or reconnaissance logic exists.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Patterns Are Project Configuration Examples
low_findings.description: Status: FALSE_POSITIVE. The path traversal hit is a Vite outDir example, and the hidden file hit is a Nuxt cache cleanup example. Neither is part of executable skill logic. Confidence: 0.9. Confidence reasoning: The lines are embedded in markdown troubleshooting examples and do not read or write files unless a user deliberately runs the shown commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem strings are project-local examples in documentation. No automated file access or traversal behavior exists in the skill.

content.value_statement: Vue 3 projects often hide type, editor, and configuration problems until late in development. This skill gives focused guidance for vue-tsc, Volar, component typing, Pinia tests, and Vue upgrade issues.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to extract Vue component prop, emit, slot, and exposed types.
Guides setup for vue-tsc strict template checks and Vue compiler options.
Provides fixes for Volar 3 editor integration and module resolution issues.
Shows patterns for typed fallthrough attributes, CSS modules, and data attributes.
Covers Pinia testing setup with Vitest and createTestingPinia.
Documents Vue 3.5 patterns for defineModel, numeric deep watch, and prop defaults.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples; it does not run tests or compilers by itself.
Some recommendations depend on Vue, Volar, TypeScript, and package versions.
It focuses on Vue 3 and may not fit Vue 2 projects.
Project-specific build systems may require additional investigation.

use_cases.title: Fix Component Type Errors
use_cases.description: Use the skill to correct props, emits, slots, and wrapper component typing in Vue 3 projects.

use_cases.title: Harden Project Type Checking
use_cases.description: Apply strict template, CSS module, fallthrough attribute, and data attribute settings during project maintenance.

use_cases.title: Resolve Upgrade Regressions
use_cases.description: Diagnose Volar 3, Vue 3.5, module resolution, Pinia testing, and typed route parameter issues.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Vue Props
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Vue 3 component and explain the best way to type its props, emits, and slots with TypeScript.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single component

prompt_templates.title: Enable Strict Templates
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me configure vue-tsc strict template checks for my Vue 3 project and explain which tsconfig file should hold the settings.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Wrapper Component Types
prompt_templates.prompt: I am building a wrapper component around an existing Vue component. Show how to preserve prop and fallthrough attribute types safely.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Vue Upgrade Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these Vue 3.5, Volar 3, router, and Pinia test errors. Prioritize root causes and propose a migration plan.

output_examples.input: A component library wrapper loses autocomplete for forwarded attributes.
output_examples.output: The response identifies the fallthrough attribute typing issue, recommends the relevant Vue compiler option, and explains how to verify editor autocomplete.

output_examples.input: A Vue project reports unknown components only at runtime.
output_examples.output: The response recommends stricter template checking, names the configuration area to inspect, and explains likely effects on existing templates.

output_examples.input: Pinia store tests fail because the testing plugin lacks a spy configuration.
output_examples.output: The response explains the required testing setup, how initial state should be supplied, and how to reset mocks between tests.

best_practices: Confirm the project Vue, TypeScript, Volar, and vue-tsc versions before applying version-specific guidance.
Prefer typed helper utilities and compiler options over manual casts when building reusable components.
Treat shell commands in troubleshooting guidance as suggestions that need project-owner review.
anti_patterns: Suppressing template type errors without documenting why the suppression is necessary.
Using broad type assertions when Vue-specific type helpers can preserve component contracts.
Copying cleanup or install commands into a shell without checking the current working directory.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code or change files?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation skill that provides Vue 3 guidance and examples for an AI assistant to use.

faq.question: Which Vue version does it target?
faq.answer: It targets Vue 3, with several recommendations for Vue 3.5 and current Vue Language Tools.

faq.question: Can it help with Volar 3 migration?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers editor integration changes, language server setup, and related TypeScript tooling issues.

faq.question: Does it replace vue-tsc or ESLint?
faq.answer: No. It explains how to configure and use those tools, but it does not replace project validation.

faq.question: Can it help with test setup?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Pinia and Vitest testing guidance, including createTestingPinia setup patterns.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk low?
faq.answer: The skill is documentation-only, but it includes shell command examples that users should review before running.
